entries.author: St. John Chrysostom
entries.title: WHERE DID THE SAMARITANS ORIGINATE?
entries.body:
At this point it is also appropriate to tell where the Samaritans originated. I say this because the entire region is called Samaria. From what source, then, did they derive this name? The mountain is called Semer from the man who had taken possession of it,{fn:0} as Isaiah also said: And the head of Samaria, Ephraim.{fn:1} The inhabitants, however, were called not Samaritans but Israelites. But as time went on, they transgressed against God, and during the reign of Pekah, Tiglath-pileser went up and seized many cities.{fn:2} After attacking and killing Elah, he gave the kingdom over to Hoshea. Later, Shalmaneser came and captured other cities and made them subject and tributary. However, though Hoshea at first yielded, he revolted afterwards from subjection and took refuge in the aid of the Ethiopians.{fn:3} The Assyrian learned this and, having made an expedition and taken them captive, forbade the nation to remain there any longer, because he suspected the possibility of another such revolt. These inhabitants, moreover, he transported to Babylon and Medea and, having brought from various regions the people dwelling in that vicinity, he caused them to dwell in Samaria so that his power might be safeguarded for the future, with loyal inhabitants in possession of the place.

When these things had taken place, God, wishing to show his power and that he had given over the Jews not because of any lack of power on his part but because of the sins of those whom he had surrendered to their enemies, sent lions on the barbarians, and these preyed on the entire nation. This was reported to the king, and he sent a certain priest to give to them the laws of God. Nevertheless, not even then were they freed entirely from their impiety, but only partly. However, as time went on they turned away from idols and worshiped God. When things had reached this point, the Jews, finally returning, showed a contentious spirit toward them as foreigners and enemies and named them Samaritans after the mountain. {fn:4}
entries.citation: Homilies on the Gospel of John 31.2

entries.author: Origen of Alexandria
entries.title: A DIFFERENT ETYMOLOGY.
entries.body: The Hebrews, however, call a guard somer, and thus they also hand on in their tradition that the Samaritans first received this name because the king of the Assyrians sent them to be guards of the land of Israel after the captivity, that is, that other Israel besides Judah, which was taken captive into Assyria because of their many sins. {fn:0} A WIDESPREAD PRACTICE OF IDOLATRY. ISHO‘DAD OF MERV: With the words from watchtower to fortified city [the Scripture] denounces the large number of their idols, that is, [there were] idols from border to border. The text refers to the fortified city as Jerusalem, but other [interpreters] say Antioch. {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
entries.title: THE ESSENTIAL SACRAMENT OF UNITY.

entries.author: Origen of Alexandria
entries.title: THE WICKEDNESS OF JUDAH AND ISRAEL.
entries.body: Faithless Israel has shown itself less guilty than false Judah.{fn:0} The letter of the text just read has something unclear that we need to understand first. Then, after this, if God wills, we shall know his mystical plan. He wants us then to know in these words, just as it is written in Kings, that the people were divided in those times into the kingdom of ten tribes under Jeroboam and the kingdom of two tribes under Roboam.{fn:1} And those under Jeroboam were called Israel, and those under Roboam Judah. And the division of the people persisted, according to the history, until today. For we know of nothing in the history that united Israel and Judah into the same nation.{fn:2} Then Israel first, under Jeroboam and under his successors, sinned excessively, and Israel sinned so much beyond Judah that they were sentenced by providence to become captives to the Assyrians until the sign,{fn:3} as the Scripture says. After this, the people of Judah also sinned, and as captives they were sentenced to Babylon, not until a sign, as Israel, but for seventy years,{fn:4} as Jeremiah prophesied and Daniel also mentioned.{fn:5} {fn:6}

entries.author: Origen of Alexandria
entries.title: THE SYMBOLIC MEANING OF THE LIONS.
entries.body: [The Lord] takes hold of two principal lions, the Assyrians and the Babylonians. According to the history in the fourth book of Kings, there are two. For Assyria removed the sons of Israel to Assyria until today, but Babylon removed the sons of Judah to Babylon.{fn:0} Except he did not say here first and second, but first and last. For the first lion is the antagonistic devil; he is a murderer.{fn:1} The very last lion at the completion of the age is the man of sin, the son of perdition, who exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship.{fn:2} {fn:3}
entries.citation: Fragments from the Catena on Jeremiah 28.2

entries.author: St. John Chrysostom
entries.title: ERRORS OF THE SAMARITANS IN WORSHIPING GOD.
entries.body: How, then, did the Samaritans not know what they worshiped? Because they thought that God was confined to a place and divisible; at least it was in that way that they worshiped him. And it was in this spirit that they sent to the Persians and announced that the God of this place was displeased with them.{fn:0} According to this, their idea of him was no greater than their conception of their idols. Therefore, they continued to worship both evil spirit and him, combining things that were altogether incompatible. But the Jews for the most part were free of this taint and knew that he is God of the universe, even though not all of them [were faithful]. {fn:1}
entries.citation: Homilies on the Gospel of John 33.1
